warnings.message: Lip condition unknown — classification halts at the OCS rollup with status INDETERMINATE. OCS is a superclass and is never emitted as a terminal code.

explanations: An open profile was selected, so the open-form families apply.
A C-form open profile was selected; lip condition decides the terminal code.
Lip condition could not be determined; the engine refuses to guess (determinism rule P7).

decision_trace.input: Is the cross-section profile open, closed (hollow), or solid? → Open profile
decision_trace.explanation: An open profile was selected, so the open-form families apply.

decision_trace.input: Which open form is it? → C-form (web + two flanges, same side)
decision_trace.explanation: A C-form open profile was selected; lip condition decides the terminal code.

decision_trace.input: Are there stiffening lips on the flange ends? → I don't know
decision_trace.standard_reference: Taxonomy Standard draft Part 3.1 (Ruling R3); Part 13.7 (W-201); principle P7
decision_trace.explanation: Lip condition could not be determined; the engine refuses to guess (determinism rule P7).

refusal.why_stopped: Terminal shape assignment requires the lip condition (it changes section behavior fundamentally); the answer given was UNKNOWN.
refusal.required_information: Inspect the flange free edges: no fold → OCU (unlipped); one fold per flange → OCL (lipped). Return-lipped profiles (OCR) are outside the Phase 1/2 demo subset.
refusal.principle: Deterministic classification SHALL refuse ambiguous terminal assignment when required input is missing (Taxonomy Standard principle P7). A guessed code would be unreproducible and untraceable.
